name: Villaverde de Pontones, Ajo, Convento de San Ildefonso – circular route
name_original: Villaverde de Pontones, Ajo, Convento de San Ildefonso – ruta circular
region_intro: No traffic road cycling routes around Lic-Rio Miera traverse the Miera Valley, a region characterized by its narrow, steep relief and diverse geological formations. The landscape is shaped by the Miera River, which carves through calcareous materials, flanked by well-preserved alder and willow groves. This area features extensive beech and holm oak forests, alongside significant karstification processes that create irregular shapes, providing varied terrain for road cyclists. Best no traffic road cycling routes around Lic-Rio Miera The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Viewpoint at Playa de Langre – Somo Beach loop from Somo, a 25.9 miles (41.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete, offering coastal views and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Section near Somo – Somo Beach loop from Somo, a moderate 23.6 miles (38.0 km) path that explores the coastal areas around Somo Beach. Local road cyclists also love the Pas Greenway (Astillero) – Pas Greenway loop from La Cantábrica, a 10.8 miles (17.4 km) trail leading through the former railway line converted into a cycle path, often completed in about 55 minutes. Road cycling around Lic-Rio Miera is defined by narrow river valleys, coastal paths, and forested sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Lic-Rio Miera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 7 reviews. More than 1900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lic-Rio Miera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic road cycling routes are available in the Lic-Rio Miera region?
A: There are 8 dedicated no-traffic road cycling routes in the Lic-Rio Miera region, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find 2 easy routes and 6 moderate routes to explore.

Q: Are there any easy, traffic-free road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, the Lic-Rio Miera region offers easy, traffic-free options perfect for beginners or families. A notable choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15001667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway (Astillero) – Pas Greenway loop from La Cantábrica&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 17.4 km route. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15031793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Campiazo loop from Bareyo&lt;/a&gt;, covering 21.8 km.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on these no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: The no-traffic road cycling routes in the Lic-Rio Miera region offer diverse and stunning scenery. You'll cycle through dramatic valleys, alongside the Miera River, and past extensive beech and holm oak forests. The region is known for its unique karst landscapes, with wild, irregular shapes formed by sinkholes and limestone pavements. Routes often provide views of the Cantabrian mountains and the coastline, especially those near Somo Beach.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic road cycling routes in the Lic-Rio Miera area?
A: Yes, many of the no-traffic road cycling routes in Lic-Rio Miera are circular. For example, you can enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32134039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint at Playa de Langre – Somo Beach loop from Somo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 41.7 km ride, or the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20518079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somo Beach loop from Somo&lt;/a&gt; at 26.8 km. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15001667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway (Astillero) – Pas Greenway loop from La Cantábrica&lt;/a&gt; is also a great circular option.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see near the no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: While cycling the no-traffic routes, you can discover several interesting landmarks and natural features. Keep an eye out for the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos III Gate&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the natural beauty of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguanaz River and Frenchman’s Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique traditional Pasiego cottages and charming towns like Liérganes with its distinctive architecture.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the no-traffic routes in Lic-Rio Miera?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in Lic-Rio Miera, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning, varied landscapes, the tranquility of the traffic-free paths, and the opportunity to immerse themselves in nature. The combination of challenging climbs and scenic descents, particularly around areas like Alto del Caracol, is frequently highlighted.

Q: Is there parking available near the starting points of these no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in the towns and villages that serve as starting points for these routes. For routes originating near Somo, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15027094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Section near Somo – Somo Beach loop from Somo&lt;/a&gt;, you'll find parking facilities in Somo itself. For routes like the Pas Greenway, look for parking in nearby towns like Astillero or La Cantábrica.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling on these traffic-free routes?
A: The Miera Valley is a great destination for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer. While the region is known for its dramatic landscapes, some higher passes might be affected by weather in winter, so checking local conditions is always recommended.

Q: Are there any specific challenges or features to be aware of on the no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: While these routes are traffic-free, the Miera Valley's topography means you can expect varied terrain. Some routes, especially those venturing into the steeper parts of the valley, may include significant elevation gains, such as the climbs towards Portillo de Lunada or Alto del Caracol, which are known for testing endurance. Always check the route's elevation profile before you go.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to rest near the no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region has charming towns and villages where you can find cafes and places to rest. Liérganes, for instance, is a picturesque town along the Miera River that offers amenities. Other villages nestled in the valley, such as San Roque de Riomiera, also provide opportunities for a break and refreshments, allowing you to refuel during your ride.
tips: Section between green meadows with a parallel bike lane separated from the road. From the bicycle you can quietly enjoy the passage through this typical area of pastures and see some herds of cows or sheep.
Small but exceptional convent, a milestone on the Camino de Santiago

Legend has it that a French abbot, fleeing the Revolution, came to these places, hiding in the cave. And they say that, from so much crying, his eyes became irritated in such a way that they caused him bad eyesight. But, on one occasion he noticed that by washing them with the waters of the fountain, his eyes healed. Hence the discovery of the virtues of the waters and the name of the place.
